highlights.text: I wrote on Friday: “Bear case Monday: Shorter-term, ES can dip on the failure of 6453”.
I wrote on Tuesday at 4pm that: “My general lean is we backtest 6461-65.
My lean was they could and I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
When it sells, it is always vertical, and “elevator down”.
Always the same thing: My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
Moreover these squeeze legs also have a fairly predictable measured move: They usually gravitate towards the zone the failure of which originally produced the “elevator down” sell.
There was the elevator down leg.
You can think of it as a rubber band being pulled back (the elevator down) then released (the short squeeze) which we saw until today.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the plethora of setups we saw this week including the monster Failed Breakdown we saw on Tuesday which triggered the 90 point leg up we saw off this weeks lows.
6428 to 6461=a range with 6438 magnet…next leg targets 6480-82, 6498, then 6505.” This tracked perfectly and we got to 6505+ by the close.

post_title: Is SPX About To Breakout To A New All Time High? Or Big Pullback First? September 5th Plan
